subject: Re: Gina Offenstine
body:
call when you come to new york

--- Cecilia Steen <cecilia.steen@gmail.com> wrote:

>  *Dear Jeffrey, it has been a very long time since I
> have written to you. I
> want to take this opportunity to tell you a bit
> about myself and my
> children. Well, some very good news. As we have all
> fallen in love with
> Boston, now I am going to buy a home. Ronnie has
> been so generous and
> understanding about how my life has been going. He
> will start his retirement
> at the end of June, as he has sold his company. So,
> I have decided to put
> my job on hold for the time being. This is because
> for the first time in my
> life I realize that I have a full time job as a
> mother, including being the
> maid, chauffer, cook, and tutor. I must say that I
> do enjoy taking care of
> my family. Can you imagine that I have 12 year old
> twins???? My daughter is
> so beautiful. She writes songs and sings like an
> angel. She is like a
> miniature Avril Levine. I am sure that she could one
> day start a career in
> the music business. My son is very smart and will
> perhaps be an architect.
> Well, I have the life of them all planned out. If
> they are anything like me,
> well, they will not ever know what is around the
> corner??? You know Jeff,
> there is hardly a day that goes by and I do not
> think about you and hope all
> is well with you. I know you are extremely busy, but
> It would be great
> to hear a word from you once in a while, just to let
> me know you are fine.
> If there is anything you need from Boston, just let
> me know. I come to NYC
> once about every month. Take care and all my best
> wishes, Gina *
> 


__________________________________________________

body_html:
call when you come to new york

--- Cecilia Steen cecilia.steen@gmail.com wrote:

*Dear Jeffrey, it has been a very long time since I
have written to you. I
want to take this opportunity to tell you a bit
about myself and my
children. Well, some very good news. As we have all
fallen in love with
Boston, now I am going to buy a home. Ronnie has
been so generous and
understanding about how my life has been going. He
will start his retirement
at the end of June, as he has sold his company. So,
I have decided to put
my job on hold for the time being. This is because
for the first time in my
life I realize that I have a full time job as a
mother, including being the
maid, chauffer, cook, and tutor. I must say that I
do enjoy taking care of
my family. Can you imagine that I have 12 year old
twins???? My daughter is
so beautiful. She writes songs and sings like an
angel. She is like a
miniature Avril Levine. I am sure that she could one
day start a career in
the music business. My son is very smart and will
perhaps be an architect.
Well, I have the life of them all planned out. If
they are anything like me,
well, they will not ever know what is around the
corner??? You know Jeff,
there is hardly a day that goes by and I do not
think about you and hope all
is well with you. I know you are extremely busy, but
It would be great
to hear a word from you once in a while, just to let
me know you are fine.
If there is anything you need from Boston, just let
me know. I come to NYC
once about every month. Take care and all my best
wishes, Gina *
